content: clippy::needless_borrow fires when you pass an already-referenced value with an extra & (for example &&str into a fn taking &str). Drop the extra borrow level — the compiler does not need it and it obscures the call site.
When every field of a PartialEq type also implements Eq, derive Eq alongside it. clippy::derive_partial_eq_without_eq flags the gap, and types used as HashMap or HashSet keys need Eq anyway.
A .clone() inside a loop where the value is only read trips clippy::redundant_clone. Bind a reference once outside the loop instead of cloning on every iteration.
Replace a match or if-let whose else arm returns or breaks with let ... else { return; }. clippy::manual_let_else flags the longer form and the let-else reads better.
